channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Developers need to balance code and health — I wasn’t, so I built this
item.description: Hey, I felt like I had no balance between coding and health. So I built a small tool where you can track workouts like GitHub commits and see your consistency as a graph. You can even do it via...

item.title: Your Pipeline Is 14.8h Behind: Catching Real Estate Sentiment Leads with Pulsebit
item.description: Your Pipeline Is 14.8h Behind: Catching Real Estate Sentiment Leads with Pulsebit We recently observed an intriguing anomaly: a 24-hour momentum spike of -0.286 in the real estate sector. This...

item.title: Apple update looks like Czech mate for locked-out iPhone user
item.description: Article URL: https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/12/ios_passcode_bug/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47737383 Points: 4 # Comments: 0

item.title: From Django Library to AWS Cognito: My Journey Building Enterprise SSO
item.description: The Client Call That Started Everything "We need SSO. Multiple providers. Per client. Starting next sprint." That was the moment I realized I had no idea what I was actually getting into. I'd...

item.title: Build a Freelancer Payout Platform with the Afriex SDK and Next.js
item.description: Paying contractors across borders means dealing with different banking rails, payment channels, and currencies simultaneously — and most existing tools either lock you into USD-first payouts or...

item.title: Roslyn-Powered C# Analysis, Real NuGet DLL Reflection, and Safe Live File Editing for Claude — Privately, On Your Machine via MCP
item.description: I built an MCP server that gives Claude surgical access to your C# codebase — locally, token-efficiently, without leaking code If you've ever watched Claude hallucinate a NuGet API that hasn't...

item.title: Flutter Error Handling with Result, introducing neverthrow_dart
item.description: Flutter Error Handling with Result in neverthrow_dart Building a robust Flutter app is hard because every layer can fail: network, storage, auth, parsing, and background work. That makes error...

item.title: AWS Cloud Practitioner Questions | RDS, Aurora, & ElastiCache
item.description: Question 1: Amazon RDS supports the following databases, EXCEPT: Answer (1): Amazon RDS does not support MongoDB. Instead, RDS supports other databases such as MySQL, MariaDB, and Microsoft SQL...

item.title: What is llms.ljson and why eCommerce needs it
item.description: AI assistants don't read your website the way customers do. They need structured, machine-readable data — and that's exactly what llms.ljson provides. If you already know about llms.txt, think of...

item.title: Building an AI Chatbot That Learns From Human Edits (Not Just Feedback)
item.description: AI is getting smarter every day. But somehow, it still feels… a little empty. It can answer questions. It can summarize books. It can even write code better than most beginners. And yet, when...

item.title: CPUID Breach Distributes STX RAT via Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to...

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621
item.description: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier...

item.title: The Netherlands is the first European country to approve Tesla’s supervised Full Self-Driving
item.description: Dutch regulators, the RDW, announced that after over a year and a half of testing, it has officially approved Tesla's Full-Self Driving (FSD) Supervised. This makes the Netherlands the first...

item.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
item.description: The OpenAI CEO's new blog post responds to both an apparent attack on his home and an in-depth New Yorker profile raising questions about his trustworthiness.

item.title: Google says Polymarket bets showing up in News was an ‘error’
item.description: Polymarket bets started popping up in Google News alongside legitimate news articles. But now those results aren't showing, and Google says they were never supposed to. Spokesperson Ned Adriance...

item.title: Pokémon Champions is off to a rough start
item.description: Like many live-service games before it, Pokémon Champions' launch has been messy. The free-to-start battle sim, which is out now on the Switch and Switch 2 (and also coming to mobile later this...

item.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
item.description: The illustration for The New Yorker's profile of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is a jump scare. Altman stands in a blue sweater with a blank expression. Around his head hovers a cluster of disembodied...

item.title: Google’s latest Nest Doorbells just hit their lowest prices of the year
item.description: If you’ve ever worried about porch pirates stealing packages while you’re away, a video doorbell can offer some peace of mind, letting you keep tabs on deliveries no matter where you are. Google...

item.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
item.description: Two weeks ago, I was getting ready to log off work when I got a text message. "Oh wow, I was checking out Mitski. did you know people are saying her Dad was a CIA operative?" Normally, that kind...

item.title: You don’t have to spend more than $50 on a great USB-C dock for your Switch 2
item.description: Nintendo seemingly designed its latest console to be a mystery for third-party accessory makers. With the Switch 2, the company changed the wireless protocol for connecting controllers to the new...

item.title: Demons and pinball are a perfect match
item.description: There's one very specific reason I keep a Wii U handy, and that's so that I have an easy way to play the classic pinball game Devil's Crush. Over the years, it has become a comfort game for me....

item.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
item.description: In the early days of the war on Iran, while the White House was busy posting Call of Duty memes and AI slop of dancing bowling pins, the Iranian regime's state media was flooding the zone with...

item.title: The new show making fun of tech bros
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 123, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, which Artemis photo did you make your wallpaper, and also you can...

item.title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
item.description: Plus: Iran’s internet blackout hits the 1,000-hour mark, cryptocurrency scams result in a record amount of money stolen from Americans, and more.

item.title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
item.description: From AI-generated images to restricted satellite data, the systems used to verify what’s real online are struggling to keep up.

item.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
item.description: Hungarian domestic intelligence, the national police in El Salvador, and several U.S. law enforcement and police departments have been attributed to the use of an advertising-based global...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Overfishing in the South Pacific
item.description: Regulation is hard: The South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organization (SPRFMO) oversees fishing across roughly 59 million square kilometers (22 million square miles) of the South...

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: The new AI model is being heralded—and feared—as a hacker’s superweapon. Experts say its arrival is a wake-up call for developers who have long made security an afterthought.

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.
